rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score of 3/10 and cite the same job-relevant gaps: no C/UNIX embedded experience, no Linux kernel modification, no embedded networking security (MACsec/802.1X), and no Japanese proficiency. The variant's added detail (web/JS focus, LAN-switch product gap) is elaboration on the same technical deficiencies, not new reasoning tied to the name change. Nothing keys off the candidate being named Aisha Okonkwo versus Bogdan Szabo.
